explicit_text: I decided to join the United States Marine Corps in 1944 because I didn't want to be in the Army or Navy. I went through boot camp at Marine Corps Recruit Depot San Diego and experienced the tough training there. During my time, I was part of the 27th, 31st, and 34th training battalions on the West Coast. The Marine Corps shaped my early adult life and gave me a sense of purpose.
implicit_text: I decided to join the branch of the military known for its tough amphibious training in 1944 because I didn't want to be in the Army or Navy. I went through boot camp at the West Coast recruit depot in San Diego and experienced the tough training there. During my time, I was part of the 27th, 31st, and 34th training battalions on the West Coast. This branch shaped my early adult life and gave me a sense of purpose.

explicit_text: My brother Marv was drafted into the United States Army right after high school graduation. He initially joined the Signal Corps, then was moved to the Tank unit, and later to the Air Corps before washing out of pilot training. Eventually, he ended up serving in Patton's Army during the North Africa Campaign. The Army was a big part of my family's military experience during World War II.
implicit_text: My brother Marv was drafted into the land forces right after high school graduation. He initially joined the communications unit, then was moved to the armored tank unit, and later to the air service before washing out of pilot training. Eventually, he ended up serving in Patton's forces during the North Africa Campaign. The land forces were a big part of my family's military experience during World War II.

explicit_text: I didn't want to join the United States Navy, so I chose the Marine Corps instead. However, I did encounter a shore patrol sailor while returning from furlough, who helped me with traveler's orders when I was three days late getting back. The Navy's shore patrol played a role in maintaining order among servicemen during that time.

explicit_text: Arnie Martin was a kid I used to play ball with, and we joined the Marine Corps together in 1944. While I was sent to San Diego for boot camp, Arnie was sent to Camp Lejeune and then to Parris Island. We started this journey together, but our paths diverged based on the Marine Corps' assignments.

entity: Marine Corps Recruit Depot San Diego
explicit_text: I went through boot camp at the Marine Corps Recruit Depot San Diego starting in August 1944. The training was rough but expected, and it prepared me for my service. After boot camp, I had a ten-day furlough to return home to Fond du Lac before returning to duty.

explicit_text: Parris Island was where Arnie Martin was sent for his Marine Corps training instead of San Diego. It was one of the main recruit depots on the East Coast. Although I didn't train there, Parris Island was a key part of the Marine Corps' training system during World War II.

explicit_text: Fond du Lac, Wisconsin, was my hometown where I spent time before joining the Marine Corps. I went to vocational school there for a year before shipping out for boot camp. After boot camp, I came back on a ten-day furlough to Fond du Lac, where my family lived and where I attended my uncle's funeral.

explicit_text: Milwaukee was where I went to take my physical exam before joining the Marine Corps. I also boarded a troop train there that took me to boot camp. It was a key transit point for me in August 1944 as I began my military service.

explicit_text: The North Africa Campaign was a major event during World War II when my brother Marv was in the Army. He ended up serving with Patton's Army after being in the Tank unit. The campaign was brutal, and the Sherman tanks they used were like tin cans, very vulnerable in battle.

The tanks were not the safest place to be, which is why he was encouraged to transfer to the Air Corps.

explicit_text: During my time in the Marine Corps, the 27th Marine Regiment was one of the training battalions on the West Coast. Many of us were out on ten-day furlough transfers while the officers were away. The 27th was part of the structure that trained Marines like me in 1944.
